review: There is no perfect service for everyone -- I've been on ten different subscription streamers since 2006. I don't care for Tidal's quirky, inflexible queue system, but the app UI works pretty well; the sliding panels were an unfamiliar design feature for me, but ended up working. But what that is really great about Tidal for my quirky tastes are the DISCOVERY options: particularly the My Daily Discovery Mixes, which give me up to 10 new tracks every day that, amazingly, fit my picky tastes.

review: This app itself is fine, I would say. It's a little buggy sometimes and doesn't offer any amazing features, but it's alright. It's other things that make TIDAL sort of iffy in my book. I have a wide variety of music tastes, and I've found that just about any artist that is even remotely considered 'underground' or lesser-known is not present on TIDAL. There's no Linux native application. Master quality audio only works on Windows. Due to the lack of Linux support as well as a lack in variety of artists, I wanted to cancel my Master quality subscription. I set a reminder to cancel my subscription the day before it was to be renewed. Getting home from work the day I was reminded, I sat down to cancel my subscription only to find that there was a pending charge on my account for the next month of my subscription, which I had assumed would go away post-cancellation. The next day the charge had gone through, even though I had cancelled my subscription. I contacted support in regards to a refund on my services as I was no longer using them. I was met with a 'sorry', no refund of my 25$, and my subscription still being cancelled. I asked to file a formal complaint, which I did, and nothing further followed. In other words, unless you're a Windows user with a pretty nice amp/DAC/headphone setup that only likes to listen to relatively popular music, stay away from the higher quality TIDAL subscriptions. I will be staying away from them from now on entirely, since it feels like they stole 25$ from me.
Palabras_Clave: subscription, offline, keeps, audio, account, issues, connection, mode, error, playlists
